reason: Core/CLI runtime quality boundaries, test expectations, and forbidden provider SDK leakage.
Provider and runtime failures must be structured and observable.
OAuth work must preserve redacted diagnostics and event-first observability.
Ink workbench surfaces must remain typed/state-driven and not parse display strings.
Workbench auth/model state should project runtime facts instead of duplicating source of truth.
UI/controller state should use discriminated unions and typed command results.
Provider auth, model registry, host factory, and router ownership model for this feature.
AI SDK stays an adapter; provider-specific auth must not leak into core contracts.
Redaction and operational diagnostics patterns for credentials.
Reference-project provider transport and credential-policy layering evidence.
